brief_title: Broadly Neutralizing Antibody Combination for HIV Reservoir Reduction
official_title: A Phase 1, Open-Label Study of a Broadly Neutralizing Antibody Combination (3BNC117 + 10-1074) for HIV-1 Reservoir Reduction in Virologically Suppressed Adults on Stable Antiretroviral Therapy
interventions: 3BNC117 30mg/kg IV infusion every 3 weeks for 3 doses
10-1074 30mg/kg IV infusion every 3 weeks for 3 doses
brief_summary: This Phase 1 study evaluates the safety and effect on the HIV-1 reservoir of a combination of two broadly neutralizing antibodies (3BNC117 and 10-1074) in virologically suppressed adults on stable antiretroviral therapy for at least 2 years. The primary endpoints are safety and change in intact proviral DNA from baseline.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Adults aged 18-60 years

2. Documented HIV-1 infection

3. On stable antiretroviral therapy for at least 2 years with no regimen changes

4. HIV-1 RNA < 50 copies/mL for at least 2 years (single blip to < 200 allowed)

5. CD4+ T-cell count > 500 cells/μL

6. CD4 nadir > 200 cells/μL

7. No documented resistance to current ART regimen

8. HIV-1 sensitivity to both 3BNC117 and 10-1074 by in vitro neutralization assay

9. Adequate venous access for IV infusions

10. Written informed consent

11. Willingness to undergo leukapheresis at baseline and week 24
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Current or recent (within 6 months) opportunistic infection

2. Active hepatitis B or C

3. History of infusion reactions to monoclonal antibodies

4. Autoimmune disease requiring immunosuppression

5. Chronic kidney disease (eGFR < 60 mL/min)

6. Active malignancy

7. Pregnant or breastfeeding

8. Prior participation in HIV cure or vaccine study within 12 months

inclusion_criteria.text: On stable antiretroviral therapy for at least 2 years with no regimen changes
HIV-1 RNA < 50 copies/mL for at least 2 years (single blip to < 200 allowed)
No documented resistance to current ART regimen
HIV-1 sensitivity to both 3BNC117 and 10-1074 by in vitro neutralization assay
Adequate venous access for IV infusions
Willingness to undergo leukapheresis at baseline and week 24

exclusion_criteria.text: Current or recent (within 6 months) opportunistic infection
History of infusion reactions to monoclonal antibodies
Prior participation in HIV cure or vaccine study within 12 months
